current_run.description: Given an N-qubit oracle that computes a boolean function f(x) = sum_{k=0}^{N-1} (x_k * s_k * x_{(k+1)%N}) mod 2, where x_k is the k-th bit of x and s_k is the k-th bit of a secret N-bit integer s. The sum is over cyclic indices (i.e., (k+1)%N handles wrapping around). The goal is to determine the secret N-bit integer s. This function is non-linear in x, and the secret s acts as a set of coefficients determining which quadratic terms contribute to the parity.
